Why This Tool Exists
Purpose of This Reader

This parallel reader places the canonical LDS text alongside a faithful modern English translation so that the meaning of every verse is immediately accessible to any reader, regardless of their familiarity with scriptural language.

Highlight verses, write annotations and personal notes, and keep a daily journal of your scripture study — all in one place.

📜
Canonical Text
The left panel shows the LDS scriptural text exactly as written.
📖
Modern Translation
The right panel gives a faithful word-for-word rendering in contemporary English.
🖊
Annotations
Highlight verses with color and add personal notes tied to each verse.
📓
Daily Journal
Keep a personal study journal with dated reflections and entries.
About the Text
Introduction to the Book of Mormon
"The Book of Mormon is a volume of holy scripture comparable to the Bible. It is a record of God's dealings with ancient inhabitants of the Americas and contains the fulness of the everlasting gospel."
— Introduction, The Book of Mormon

The Book of Mormon follows multiple narrative threads: the family of Lehi, who fled Jerusalem before its Babylonian conquest and sailed to the Americas; the civilizations their descendants built; the ministry of Jesus Christ among these people following his resurrection; and the final collapse of Nephite civilization, preserved by the prophet Mormon and his son Moroni.

About This App

This reader was built with one purpose at its heart: to help every person who wants to understand the Book of Mormon actually understand it — verse by verse, word by word, line by line.

The Book of Mormon is, above all else, a testament of Jesus Christ. Every major prophet in its pages — Nephi, Jacob, Alma, Helaman, Mormon, Moroni — wrote to witness of Him. The wars and migrations and political crises are backdrop. The doctrine of Christ is the point. This app is designed to keep that center visible.

The parallel translation places the canonical LDS scriptural text alongside a faithful modern English rendering — verse by verse. Not a paraphrase, not a simplification: a careful attempt to let the meaning of each verse come through in language that doesn't require decoding. Readers who grew up with the text will find the modern column illuminating. Readers coming to the Book of Mormon for the first time will find it welcoming.

The Study Helps for each chapter — summaries, themes, notable verses, cross-references, and questions to ponder — are written to be brief enough to read before or after a chapter and substantial enough to actually deepen your study. Every question is meant to be sat with, not answered quickly.

Use the highlighter to mark verses that speak to you. Add verse notes tied to specific passages. Keep a journal of your study — dated reflections you can return to. Track your reading progress through all fifteen books. Everything saves automatically to your device.

This is not an official product of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. It is an independent labor of love — made for the person who wants to take the Book of Mormon seriously and needs tools that help them do that.

Wherever you are in your study — beginning, returning, or lifelong — you are welcome here. The promise in Moroni 10:4 holds: ask with a sincere heart, with real intent, and the answer will come. This app is simply one small tool to help you do that asking.

Book of Mormon Parallel Reader · Independent Study Tool · Not an Official Church Publication
